essence: Maintains, tests, and repairs the critical equipment used by emergency services to ensure readiness and safety.

identity.title: Emergency Equipment Technician
identity.summary: Inspects, services, and repairs emergency‑service equipment such as radios, PPE, breathing apparatus, medical kits, and rescue tools.

salary.notes: Specialists in breathing apparatus, communications systems, or advanced rescue tools can exceed £45k–£55k+.

qualifications: Technical training in equipment maintenance or engineering
Knowledge of emergency‑service tools and PPE standards
Ability to test, calibrate, and certify safety‑critical equipment
Understanding of health and safety and inspection protocols

lifestyle.description: Emergency equipment technicians work in workshops, depots, or stations maintaining essential tools such as breathing apparatus, radios, medical kits, and rescue equipment. The role involves inspections, repairs, record‑keeping, and ensuring everything meets safety standards. It suits individuals who enjoy technical work, precision, and supporting emergency responders behind the scenes.

habits: Follows strict safety and certification procedures
Maintains a clean and organised workshop
